top_categories.workout_context: 10,023ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
36 miles on paved Hawaiian volcanic mountain road burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala's paved Hawaiian volcanic mountain road forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 36 miles.
Base fitness for Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
278ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Hawaiian volcanic mountain road. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,023ft of climbing on paved Hawaiian volcanic mountain road does to your power file on race day.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 36 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.
At Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Cycle to the Sun — Haleakala with 10,023ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. 40-degree Fahrenheit temperature drop from start to summit; Summit wind chill can feel below freezing even in summer.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 36 miles of paved hawaiian volcanic mountain road in Pa'ia, Maui, Hawaii.
